title: MBT Workspace Description
description: Concisely describes the information a language server needs to interpret a workspace. mbt.json files can be genarated from many build systems like Maven, Bazel, Gradle, etc.

dependencyModules.description: Collection of all resolved external dependency artifacts available to the build graph.

id.description: Stable dependency identifier. For JVM builds this is commonly a coordinate-like string, but consumers should treat it as an opaque MBT dependency ID.

jar.description: URI to the resolved binary artifact. Currently, only file URIs are supported

sources.description: URI to the dependency's source artifact when available. Currently, only file URIs are supported

namespaces.description: Map of MBT namespaces keyed by namespace identifier. A namespace represents a compilable build unit such as a module, target, source set, package group, or test scope.

propertyNames.description: Namespace identifier. Usually derived from the originating build system and stable within the workspace.

sources.description: Source directories belonging to this namespace.

items.description: Workspace-relative path to a source root. Always uses forward slashes

scalacOptions.description: Scala compiler options applied when compiling this namespace.

javacOptions.description: Java compiler options applied when compiling this namespace.

dependencyModules.description: List of external dependency IDs required by this namespace. Values reference entries in the top-level dependencyModules array.

javaHome.description: Filesystem path to the Java installation used to compile or analyze this namespace.

dependsOn.description: Ids of other MBT namespaces that this namespace depends on. These represent intra-workspace relationships rather than external dependencies.

uncheckedSources.description: Paths to generated source directories or .srcjar archives compiled along with "sources" of this namespace. Unlike "sources", those can come from `target` or otherwise git-ignored directories.
Paths to generated source directories or .srcjar archives, compiled alongside all git-ls-found workspace sources for files not scoped via namespaces.
